name: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
description: Per-user multi-factor authentication for Jellyfin: TOTP authenticator enrollment with single-use recovery codes, enforced at sign-in for enrolled users.
overview: Per-user TOTP multi-factor authentication with recovery codes for Jellyfin.

versions.changelog: v1.0.0 — Initial release. Per-user multi-factor authentication for Jellyfin: TOTP authenticator enrollment plus single-use recovery codes, always enforced at sign-in for enrolled users, with a minimal admin page to view enrollment status and reset/disable a locked-out user. Enforcement is race-free: native Jellyfin login endpoints (/Users/AuthenticateByName and /Users/{id}/Authenticate) are refused with 403 BEFORE a session token is minted for any user who must satisfy 2FA, so no password-only token is ever usable; a SessionStarted failsafe backs up paths that carry no username pre-auth (e.g. Quick Connect). Users with no 2FA obligation are unaffected and sign in normally on every client. Security: TOTP secrets are AES-256-GCM encrypted and AAD-bound to the user id; recovery codes are PBKDF2-SHA256 salted; the encryption key file is locked down with Unix 0600 and an explicit restrictive Windows ACL; disabling/resetting 2FA revokes the user's live sessions. Targets .NET 8 and ABI 10.10.0.0 (built against Jellyfin.Controller/Model 10.10.6; loads on any 10.10.x server including 10.10.7).
